issue.title: Accessibility issue: Banner must be a <section> element or have role="region" on /a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html
Accessibility issue: Banner section landmark missing accessible name on /a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html

findings.problemShort: Banner must be a <section> element or have role="region"
findings.solutionShort: Use <section> element (implicit role) or add explicit role="region"
findings.solutionLong:
The banner element <div> does not have the correct landmark role. Banner components must use a <section> element (which has an implicit role="region") or have an explicit role="region" attribute to be properly exposed as section landmarks for screen reader users.

TARGET_SELECTOR: `banner, [data-testid="banner"]`
TARGET_INDEX: 0

**Scanned page:** [https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html](https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html)

findings.problemShort: Banner section landmark missing accessible name
findings.solutionShort: Add aria-label or aria-labelledby to provide context
findings.solutionLong:
The banner element section[data-testid="banner"] is a section landmark, but does not have an accessible name. Section landmarks (role="region") require aria-label or aria-labelledby to provide context about the banner type (e.g., "Critical alert", "Info notification") for screen reader users.

TARGET_SELECTOR: `banner, [data-testid="banner"]`
TARGET_INDEX: 1

**Scanned page:** [https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html](https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html)

issue.title: Accessibility issue: Banner icon must be marked as decorative with aria-hidden="true" on /a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html

findings.problemShort: Banner icon must be marked as decorative with aria-hidden="true"
findings.solutionShort: Add aria-hidden="true" to the SVG icon element
findings.solutionLong:
The banner section[data-testid="banner"] contains an SVG icon without aria-hidden="true". Banner type indicator icons are decorative because the banner type is conveyed through the accessible name and heading. Exposing the icon to screen readers creates redundant announcements. Add aria-hidden="true" to the SVG to hide it from assistive technologies. This relates to WCAG 2.1 Success Criteria 1.1.1 (Non-text Content) and 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).

TARGET_SELECTOR: `banner, [data-testid="banner"]`
TARGET_INDEX: 2
TARGET_SELECTOR_MODE: svg

**Scanned page:** [https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html](https://grace-snow.github.io/a11y-scan-plugins/pages/banner-test-broken.html)
